title: TLS, SSE-KMS, Bucket Keys, and Key Operations · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A TypeScript pairing room incrementally builds encryption and key-lifecycle contract, validates one changed runtime input, and keeps earlier HTTP, accessibility, security, stream, AbortSignal, and resource-budget rules active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of encryption and key-lifecycle contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport At Rest Client
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7518 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate TLS, server encryption, KMS policy, context, and rotation evidence; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport At Rest Client.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, separate tls in transit, s3 server-side encryption, kms envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Transport At Rest Client, keep Transport At Rest Client connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3282 during a private media entitlement change. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate TLS, server encryption, KMS policy, context, and rotation evidence; bound bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and spend; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, prove new writes, old reads, role rotation, key disable recovery, compromised-session revocation limits, and residual cached or signed access.

Inspect the Key Rotation Revocation Drill trace for this workshop case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Audit the proposed evidence packet · Workshop Key Rotation Revocatio….

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Audit the proposed evidence packet · Workshop Key Rotation Revocatio…, keep Key Rotation Revocation Drill connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Key Rotation Revocation Dr…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: storage-ts-key-rotation-revocation-drill-1tiaga" that uses case 6462 to separate TLS, server encryption, KMS policy, context, and rotation evidence. Guided rehearsal: predict the branch, make one bounded increment, and inspect peer-readable changed output. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node or AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, networks, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the TypeScript 7.0.2 native compiler and the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 compiler that reports 6.0.3, Node 24.18.0, AWS SDK v3.1087.0, controlled AWS, stream and handle, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
